title: KV-Tracker: Real-Time Pose Tracking with Transformers
summary: Multi-view 3D geometry networks offer a powerful prior but are prohibitively slow for real-time use. KV-Tracker adapts them for online operation, enabling real-time 6-DoF pose tracking and online reconstruction of objects and scenes from monocular RGB video: it rapidly selects and manages keyframes to map a scene or object via π³ with full bidirectional attention, then caches the global self-attention block's key-value (KV) pairs and uses them as the sole scene representation for online tracking. This yields up to 15× inference speedup without drift or catastrophic forgetting, and the caching strategy is model-agnostic, applying to other off-the-shelf multi-view networks without retraining. On TUM RGB-D, 7-Scenes, Arctic and OnePose, KV-Tracker performs strongly on both scene-level tracking and the more challenging depth-free, prior-free on-the-fly object tracking and reconstruction, while maintaining frame-rates up to ~27 FPS.
